description: The build loop leaves every project with a working, documented local preview as its default outcome, so a non-coding owner always has a runtime they can drive and review without a cloud account or a deploy step.
iWant: every build to leave me a working, documented way to run the app locally
soThat: I can see and use what was built without needing to deploy it anywhere first
title: Every build leaves a working local preview

acceptanceCriteria.description: A build's definition of done requires a documented local preview loop as the default outcome
acceptanceCriteria.given: the RCF build method as taught by the shipped guidance pack
acceptanceCriteria.when: a build reaches its definition of done
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance requires a working, documented local preview loop (a dev server, and seeded data where the app needs it) to be present as the default deliverable

acceptanceCriteria.description: The local preview is startable via a single documented command where the stack allows
acceptanceCriteria.given: the shipped build guidance describing the local preview
acceptanceCriteria.when: it states how the owner starts the app
acceptanceCriteria.then: it directs the build toward one documented command to start the preview wherever the stack makes a single command possible

acceptanceCriteria.description: The local preview is produced whether or not a hosting target was stated
acceptanceCriteria.given: an owner who has not stated a hosting target, or who asked for a recommendation instead
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance still requires the local preview to be produced as the default, not made conditional on a stated host

acceptanceCriteria.description: The preview includes seeded data when the app needs data to be usable
acceptanceCriteria.given: an application that needs data present to be exercisable
acceptanceCriteria.when: the local preview loop is produced
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance requires seeded or sample data so the preview can actually be driven, not an empty shell
